name: Sustainable Finance Disclosure Regulation
description: The SFDR establishes harmonised rules for financial market participants and financial advisers on transparency regarding the integration of sustainability risks, the consideration of adverse sustainability impacts in their processes, and the provision of sustainability-related information with respect to financial products.

article6.name: Article 6 — Sustainability risk integration
article6.description: Products that integrate sustainability risks into investment decisions but do not promote environmental or social characteristics and do not have sustainable investment as their objective.
requirements: Integrate sustainability risks into investment decision-making process
Describe manner in which sustainability risks are integrated
Describe likely impacts of sustainability risks on returns
Where sustainability risks are deemed not relevant, provide clear and concise explanation

article8.name: Article 8 — Promotion of environmental/social characteristics
article8.description: Products that promote, among other characteristics, environmental or social characteristics, or a combination of those characteristics, provided that the companies in which the investments are made follow good governance practices.
requirements: Disclose how environmental or social characteristics are met
If an index is designated as a reference benchmark, describe how the index is consistent with those characteristics
Indicate where information on the methodology for the calculation of the index can be found
Pre-contractual disclosures per Annex II of RTS
Periodic disclosures per Annex IV of RTS

article8.name: Article 8 (light green)
article8.description: Promotes E/S characteristics but does not commit to minimum proportion of sustainable investments

article8Plus.name: Article 8+ (dark green)
article8Plus.description: Promotes E/S characteristics AND commits to making a minimum proportion of sustainable investments (as defined under Article 2(17) SFDR)

article9.name: Article 9 — Sustainable investment objective
article9.description: Products that have sustainable investment as their objective. Where an index has been designated as a reference benchmark, that index must be consistent with the sustainable investment objective.
requirements: Disclose how the designated index is aligned with the sustainable investment objective
Explain why and how the index differs from a broad market index
Pre-contractual disclosures per Annex III of RTS
Periodic disclosures per Annex V of RTS
Where no EU Climate Transition Benchmark or EU Paris-Aligned Benchmark exists, provide detailed explanation of how the sustainable investment objective is achieved

principalAdverseImpacts.description: Principal adverse impacts (PAI) of investment decisions on sustainability factors. Financial market participants shall publish and maintain on their websites a statement on due diligence policies with respect to the principal adverse sustainability impacts of investment decisions.
principalAdverseImpacts.referenceDate: Calculated as at 31 December each year, published by 30 June following year
principalAdverseImpacts.lookBackPeriod: Current reporting period + comparison with previous 3 periods once available

climateAndEnvironment.name: GHG emissions

metrics.name: Scope 1 GHG emissions
metrics.definition: Scope 1 GHG emissions generated by investee companies
metrics.formula: For each investee company i: Scope1_i. Portfolio total = SUM_i(attribution_factor_i * Scope1_i), where attribution_factor_i = current_value_investment_i / enterprise_value_i

metrics.name: Scope 2 GHG emissions
metrics.definition: Scope 2 GHG emissions generated by investee companies
metrics.formula: For each investee company i: Scope2_i. Portfolio total = SUM_i(attribution_factor_i * Scope2_i), where attribution_factor_i = current_value_investment_i / enterprise_value_i

metrics.name: Scope 3 GHG emissions
metrics.definition: Scope 3 GHG emissions generated by investee companies
metrics.formula: For each investee company i: Scope3_i. Portfolio total = SUM_i(attribution_factor_i * Scope3_i), where attribution_factor_i = current_value_investment_i / enterprise_value_i

metrics.name: Total GHG emissions
metrics.definition: Total GHG emissions (Scope 1 + Scope 2 + Scope 3) generated by investee companies
metrics.formula: Total = Scope1 + Scope2 + Scope3 (from first reporting period onwards, including Scope 3 from 1 January 2023)

climateAndEnvironment.name: Carbon footprint
climateAndEnvironment.definition: Carbon footprint of the portfolio, defined as total GHG emissions per million EUR invested
climateAndEnvironment.formula: Carbon_footprint = SUM_i(attribution_factor_i * GHG_total_i) / current_value_of_all_investments_EUR_millions, where attribution_factor_i = current_value_investment_i / enterprise_value_i

climateAndEnvironment.name: GHG intensity of investee companies
climateAndEnvironment.definition: Weighted average GHG intensity of investee companies, per million EUR of revenue
climateAndEnvironment.formula: GHG_intensity = SUM_i(weight_i * (GHG_total_i / revenue_i)), where weight_i = current_value_investment_i / current_value_all_investments

climateAndEnvironment.name: Exposure to companies active in the fossil fuel sector
climateAndEnvironment.metric: Share of investments in companies active in the fossil fuel sector
climateAndEnvironment.definition: Share of investments in companies active in the fossil fuel sector, including extraction, processing, storage and transportation of fossil fuels
fossilFuelActivities: Exploration, mining, extraction, production, processing, storage, refining or distribution of hard coal and lignite
Exploration, extraction, production, processing, storage, refining or distribution of liquid fossil fuels
Exploration, extraction, manufacturing, processing, storage, refining or distribution of gaseous fossil fuels
Generation of electricity from fossil fuel sources

climateAndEnvironment.name: Share of non-renewable energy consumption and production
climateAndEnvironment.metric: Share of non-renewable energy consumption and non-renewable energy production of investee companies from non-renewable energy sources compared to renewable energy sources, expressed as a percentage of total energy sources

climateAndEnvironment.name: Energy consumption intensity per high impact climate sector
climateAndEnvironment.metric: Energy consumption in GWh per million EUR of revenue of investee companies, per high impact climate sector (NACE Sections A-H, L)
climateAndEnvironment.formula: For each NACE sector s: Energy_intensity_s = SUM_i_in_s(weight_i * energy_consumption_i / revenue_i), where weight_i = current_value_investment_i / current_value_all_investments_in_sector_s

highImpactSectors.name: Electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply
Water supply; sewerage, waste management and remediation activities
Wholesale and retail trade; repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles

climateAndEnvironment.name: Activities negatively affecting biodiversity-sensitive areas
climateAndEnvironment.metric: Share of investments in investee companies with sites/operations located in or near to biodiversity-sensitive areas where activities of those investee companies negatively affect those areas
biodiversityAreas: Other areas under the Biodiversity Convention

climateAndEnvironment.name: Emissions to water
climateAndEnvironment.metric: Tonnes of emissions to water generated by investee companies per million EUR invested, expressed as a weighted average

climateAndEnvironment.name: Hazardous waste and radioactive waste ratio
climateAndEnvironment.metric: Tonnes of hazardous waste and radioactive waste generated by investee companies per million EUR invested, expressed as a weighted average
climateAndEnvironment.formula: Hazardous_waste_ratio = SUM_i(attribution_factor_i * hazardous_waste_i) / current_value_of_all_investments_EUR_millions, where attribution_factor_i = current_value_investment_i / enterprise_value_i

climateAndEnvironment.name: Violations of UN Global Compact principles and OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises
climateAndEnvironment.metric: Share of investments in investee companies that have been involved in violations of the UNGC principles or OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises

climateAndEnvironment.name: Lack of processes and compliance mechanisms to monitor compliance with UN Global Compact principles and OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises
climateAndEnvironment.metric: Share of investments in investee companies without policies to monitor compliance with the UNGC principles or OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises or grievance/complaints handling mechanisms to address violations

climateAndEnvironment.name: Unadjusted gender pay gap
climateAndEnvironment.metric: Average unadjusted gender pay gap of investee companies
climateAndEnvironment.formula: Gender_pay_gap = SUM_i(weight_i * unadjusted_gender_pay_gap_i), where weight_i = current_value_investment_i / current_value_all_investments. Unadjusted gender pay gap = (avg_male_pay - avg_female_pay) / avg_male_pay * 100

climateAndEnvironment.name: Board gender diversity
climateAndEnvironment.metric: Average ratio of female to male board members in investee companies, expressed as a percentage of all board members
climateAndEnvironment.formula: Board_diversity = SUM_i(weight_i * (female_board_members_i / total_board_members_i * 100)), where weight_i = current_value_investment_i / current_value_all_investments

climateAndEnvironment.name: Exposure to controversial weapons
climateAndEnvironment.metric: Share of investments in investee companies involved in the manufacture or selling of controversial weapons (anti-personnel mines, cluster munitions, chemical weapons and biological weapons)

sovereignAndSupranational.name: GHG intensity
sovereignAndSupranational.formula: Sovereign_GHG_intensity = SUM_i(weight_i * (GHG_emissions_country_i / GDP_country_i)), where weight_i = current_value_investment_i / current_value_all_sovereign_investments

sovereignAndSupranational.name: Investee countries subject to social violations
sovereignAndSupranational.metric: Number of investee countries subject to social violations (absolute number and relative number divided by all investee countries), as referred to in international treaties and conventions, United Nations principles and, where applicable, national law

realEstate.name: Exposure to fossil fuels through real estate assets
realEstate.metric: Share of investments in real estate assets involved in the extraction, storage, transport or manufacture of fossil fuels

realEstate.name: Exposure to energy-inefficient real estate assets
realEstate.metric: Share of investments in energy-inefficient real estate assets
realEstate.formula: RE_energy_inefficient = SUM(value_of_energy_inefficient_real_estate_assets) / current_value_of_all_real_estate_investments * 100. Energy-inefficient = EPC below C (or in top 30% of national energy consumption for non-EPC jurisdictions)

climateAndEnvironment.name: Emissions of inorganic pollutants
Emissions of air pollutants
Emissions of ozone depletion substances
Investments in companies without carbon emission reduction initiatives
Breakdown of energy consumption by type of non-renewable sources
Water usage and recycling
Investments in companies without water management policies
Exposure to areas of high water stress
Investments in companies producing chemicals
Land degradation, desertification, soil sealing
Investments in companies without sustainable land/agriculture practices
Investments in companies without sustainable oceans/seas practices
Non-recycled waste ratio
Natural species and protected areas
Deforestation
Share of securities not certified as green under future EU acts
Share of energy from nuclear sources
Share of energy from non-renewable sources used by real estate assets
GHG emissions per square meter of real estate assets
Exposure to fossil fuels through real estate assets (additional breakdown)
Investments in real estate without consideration of energy efficiency
Average energy efficiency score of real estate assets
Share of bonds not certified as green
Carbon emissions per square meter for high-impact sectors

socialAndGovernance.name: Investments in companies without workplace accident prevention policies
Rate of accidents
Number of days lost to injuries, accidents, fatalities or illness
Lack of a supplier code of conduct
Lack of grievance/complaints handling mechanism related to employee matters
Insufficient whistleblower protection
Incidents of discrimination
Excessive CEO pay ratio
Lack of a human rights policy
Lack of due diligence
Lack of processes and measures for preventing trafficking in human beings
Operations and suppliers at significant risk of incidents of child labour
Operations and suppliers at significant risk of incidents of forced or compulsory labour
Number of identified cases of severe human rights issues and incidents
Lack of anti-corruption and anti-bribery policies
Cases of insufficient action taken to address breaches of standards of anti-corruption and anti-bribery
Number of convictions and amount of fines for violation of anti-corruption and anti-bribery laws
Average income inequality score
Average freedom of expression score
Average human rights performance
Average rule of law score
Average corruption score

preContractual.description: Disclosures to be included in pre-contractual documents (prospectus, KIID, etc.)

requiredSections: No significant harm to any sustainable investment objective
Attainment of the sustainable investment objective

periodic.description: Annual disclosures in periodic reports of the financial product

requiredSections: Extent to which E/S characteristics were met
Actions taken to meet E/S characteristics
How the reference benchmark was used (if applicable)
Extent to which sustainable investment objective was attained
Actions taken to attain the sustainable investment objective
How the reference benchmark was used

website.description: Ongoing disclosures on the financial market participant's website
article10Requirements: Description of the E/S characteristics or sustainable investment objective
Information on methodologies used to assess, measure and monitor

entityLevel.description: Entity-level disclosures required of financial market participants
entityLevel.article3: Integration of sustainability risks in investment decisions — policies published on website
entityLevel.article4: Adverse sustainability impacts — statement on website (comply or explain for entities with <500 employees)
entityLevel.article5: Sustainability risks in remuneration policies — information on website
